title: azorella haastii subsp. cyanopetala
intro: Azorella haastii subsp. cyanopetala is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Azorella haastii subsp. cyanopetala include its potential to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
